task_completion.description: A Line markup (ruler) is created in the scene
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line with control points is visible in the 2D slice views (Red, Yellow, or Green) or the 3D view.

task_completion.description: Line is placed from frontal to occipital lobe
task_completion.visual_evidence: The line endpoints are positioned at the anterior (front) and posterior (back) of the brain tissue, spanning the brain parenchyma.
The module panel on the left shows the 'Line Profile' module title or interface.

task_completion.description: Correct input line and volume selected
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Line Profile module, 'Input line' is set to the created markup and 'Input volume' is set to 'MRHead'.

task_completion.description: Intensity profile graph is computed and visible
task_completion.visual_evidence: A plot or graph window is visible showing a curve representing intensity values (Y-axis) over distance (X-axis).

task_completion.description: Profile data is saved to CSV
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog is visible pointing to '~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/', or a file browser shows 'line_profile_output.csv' in that directory.

integrity.description: Agent uses the Slicer GUI for operations
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Slicer menus, toolbars, and module panels rather than command-line scripts.

integrity.description: Agent does not manually type data into a text file
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editor is seen where the agent is manually typing intensity values or distance data.

integrity.description: Result is derived from the software computation
integrity.visual_evidence: The intensity graph/table appears within the Slicer interface before the file is saved.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The MRHead volume has a verified intensity range of 0 to 279. A valid line profile through the brain should have a length between 80mm and 200mm.
